justification: All pass criteria met: three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts), CouponRepository defined as an interface, CouponService injects the repository, all 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, plus AC1 calculation), tests use mock repository and cover all 5 ACs with explicit test names, files_changed lists all three files with purposes, and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific test names.

observations.text: All three files created with correct separation of concerns per MJ's architecture spec
CouponRepository explicitly defined as an interface with getCoupon(code: string): Coupon | null signature
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly: COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET
AC5 (single-use check) correctly implemented — a common omission that was not missed here
Tests use mock CouponRepository with no real database calls
4 additional edge-case tests beyond the 5 required ACs (fractional totals, boundary at minimumOrder, inverse single-use, multi-use with used=true)
AC1 tested with specific values and calculation verification (cartTotal * 0.90)
Guard ordering listed as null → expired → minimumOrder → singleUse, which differs slightly from the expected order (existence → expiry → single-use → minimum order), though all checks are present
